moon_lore: The city's sky is overcast 11 months of the year with corp atmospheric reclaimers and ad-blimps; the moon is folklore. The shift-cycle clock that runs everything is governed by the City Grid Authority and resets at 00:00 Neon-Dawn every day.
biome_note: Cyber has one biome (megacity) but five vertical strata: surface_slum (street level + 5m), corp_tier (5-300m), sky_arcology (300m-1km), maglev_corridor (sub-surface 0--40m), and deep_tunnels (-40m and below). Temperature varies more by stratum than by season — corp_tier is climate-controlled 22°C year-round; surface_slum tracks ambient with 5-15°C corp-exhaust uplift; deep_tunnels run hot from server farms.

day_phases.label: Neon Dawn
day_phases.cultural_notes: Night-shift workers stream home; day-shift commute begins. Ramen stalls open; corp turnstiles spin up.

day_phases.label: Corp Hours
day_phases.cultural_notes: Office towers visibly lit. Drone delivery peak. Hacking activity drops 40% (more eyes on monitors). Insurance rates highest.

day_phases.label: Gray Market
day_phases.cultural_notes: Lunch-into-afternoon window. Unauthorized chrome shops 'soft open'. Most data brokerage deals happen here in numbered crow-bars below ad-screens.

day_phases.label: Chrome Hours
day_phases.cultural_notes: Aug clinics fully operational. Street-level violence rises. The corps clock out; ICE stays vigilant.

day_phases.label: Dark Hours
day_phases.cultural_notes: Most aggressive ICE deployments hunt deckers in the deep tunnels. Surface goes quiet except corp patrols and aug-predators. Highest mortality window per capita.

months.label: Neogen-Prime
months.cultural_notes: First month after the annual Grid Reset. Tax-quarter rollover; corp loyalty bonuses paid.

months.label: Ruststorm
months.cultural_notes: Acid rain peaks; surface_slum residents shelter for 14h shifts. Anti-corrosion masks at premium.

months.label: Blackice
months.cultural_notes: ICE manufacturers run product launches. Hardest month for unaugmented deckers.

months.label: Thaw
months.cultural_notes: Surface_slum repair window. Markets open longer.

months.label: Static
months.cultural_notes: ESD events common in deep_tunnels; server farms ratchet up cooling.

months.label: Neonbloom
months.cultural_notes: Festival month. Ad-blimps in fleet formation; corp PR events.

months.label: Smog
months.cultural_notes: Surface air quality below 50 AQI for 12+ hours/day. Aug clinics see respiratory-implant peak.

months.label: Scorch
months.cultural_notes: Heat-stroke deaths peak in surface_slum. Coolant-trade gangs control distribution.

months.label: Monsoon
months.cultural_notes: Two-week storms. Tunnel flooding kills hundreds of deep-tunnel residents annually.

months.label: Drydust
months.cultural_notes: Particulate season. Wind off the wasteland. Filter-trade peaks.

months.label: Windgrip
months.cultural_notes: Wind-shear month. Ad-blimps grounded. Sky_arcology residents complain about the silence.

months.label: Longshade
months.cultural_notes: Sun obscured most days. Solar-collector arcologies drop production 30%.

months.label: Frostgrid
months.cultural_notes: Last month of the year. Grid Authority audits. Outstanding tax-debts come due; some pay in chrome.

notes: Cyber has no astronomical year — only the City Grid Authority's accounting year. The 364-day cycle exists for corp tax purposes.
Hour 0 doesn't exist — the day starts at hour 1 (Neon Dawn 01:00). This is a Grid Authority artefact: no day begins at 'nothing.'
Cross-world note: cyber's 28-hour day means a tunyan visitor (32-hour day) feels jet-lagged for ~3 days; a fantasy visitor (24h) feels persistently 'short on time' until they adjust.
